metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define or cite the 8 dso red-team recall patterns as an explicit, reachable vocabulary in the plan (inline, as a named file, or as a pointer to an existing artifact in the repo), so an executor can produce the overlay content without guessing.
findings.finding: The 8 dso red-team recall patterns (authz completeness, taint flow to sinks, fail-open error handling, privilege escalation via side effects, crypto misuse, TOCTOU, trust boundaries, state-machine integrity) are referenced as the content vocabulary for the overlay but are defined nowhere in the repo — not in code-review-security.md, not in any eval spec, not in any fixture file.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a subsection to the plan (or directly to code-review-security.md) enumerating each of the 8 concern patterns with a one-line description and at least one concrete code-pattern example per concern, so the recall vocabulary is self-contained.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define quantitative pass thresholds for AC2: specify the required recall rate (e.g. all 8 DSO concerns must have ≥1 fixture that fires) and the maximum tolerated FP-suppression count (e.g. 0 valid-pattern fixtures may be suppressed) so the eval result is deterministically pass/fail.
findings.finding: AC2 ('passing recall + no-fire eval') never defines what 'passing' means quantitatively — no recall threshold (e.g. 8/8 patterns triggered, ≥N fixture hits) and no no-fire threshold (e.g. 0 FP fires on N guard cases) are stated. An executor cannot determine whether a run constitutes a pass or fail without inventing these thresholds.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC2: 'Passing = all 8 DSO red-team concern classes have ≥1 fixture that triggers the overlay; 0 no-fire guard fixtures may suppress a pattern that a human reviewer would flag. Eval run command: `make eval-security` exits 0.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md section documenting the 'domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic verifier' invariant, so the boundary is preserved for future contributors.
findings.finding: The plan asserts a 'locked principle' (domain rubric lives in the overlay, not code-review-verify.md) but does not plan to capture this architectural decision in an ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design doc, leaving it undocumented for future contributors.
findings.location: ## What / AC: 'Overlay remains advisory; no domain rubric leaks into code-review-verify.md.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR (e.g., docs/adr/005-security-overlay-rubric-boundary.md) or a CLAUDE.md entry stating: 'Security domain rubric (recall patterns, FP-guards, 8 red-team concerns) belongs exclusively in code-review-security.md; code-review-verify.md must remain domain-agnostic.' Reference this from the overlay file header.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the DET deferral rule directly to code-review-security.md (e.g. an instruction stating that secret-detection and high-critical-security findings already handled by gitleaks/opengrep detectors should not be re-raised as LLM findings), co-located with the overlay's processing instructions.
findings.finding: The decision rule 'hard exclusions defer to the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria BY NAME' is defined only in the plan's What section but is not co-located with the overlay prompt (code-review-security.md) that the executing model will read. Without this rule in the prompt itself, the model has no instruction to defer to the deterministic detectors and may raise duplicate findings.
findings.location: Plan 'What' section ('Hard exclusions defer to the gitleaks/opengrep DET criteria BY NAME') vs. src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-security.md
findings.suggested_fix: Add a section to code-review-security.md: 'Hard exclusions: do NOT raise findings for bare secret values or High/Critical OWASP-CWE patterns already covered by the `secret-detection` and `high-critical-security` deterministic detectors. These are handled by the fail-closed gitleaks/opengrep gate.'

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the quantitative 'passing' threshold for the recall + no-fire eval in AC2 (e.g. minimum recall rate across 8 concern fixtures, maximum false-fire rate, and the gate expression such as `at_least(k)`).
findings.finding: AC2 states the change 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval' but never defines what 'passing' means: no recall threshold (e.g. ≥7/8 concerns triggered), no false-fire rate bound, and no gate expression (the established pattern is `gate: at_least(k)` as in code-quality.eval.yaml). This is an undefined decision rule the eval harness must apply.
findings.location: Plan AC line: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Specify in AC2 or the eval spec stub: recall ≥ N/8 concerns triggered (suggest N=7), false-fire rate ≤ X% on no-fire fixtures, gate: at_least(2) epochs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the complete FP-guard vocabulary in the plan (all rationalizations-to-reject, not just the one example), so the overlay content and the no-fire eval fixtures can be systematically derived.
findings.finding: The FP-discrimination guard vocabulary is referenced ('FP-guards', 'rationalizations-to-reject: not directly exploitable => not a finding') but is never concretely specified: no list of guarded FP patterns, no instruction form, and no co-location with the recall pattern section in the overlay. An executor cannot write the FP-guard content without knowing what additional rationalization patterns to reject.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the external consumers of `code-review-security.md` (`code-review.yaml`, `test_code_review_ws4.py`, `test_code_review_workflow.py`) in the plan and confirm whether the enrichment's contract changes require any of them to be updated.
findings.finding: Three consumers of `code-review-security.md` outside `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/` are unenumerated: `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/code-review.yaml` (references the prompt by id at lines 72 and 100), `tests/unit/test_code_review_ws4.py` (lines 154, 168), and `tests/unit/workflow/test_code_review_workflow.py` (lines 111, 318, 322). If the overlay's output contract, frontmatter fields, or dimension tag change, these consumers may need updating.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a described verification step for AC3 — e.g., a diff/grep assertion that code-review-verify.md is not modified, or an explicit note that the eval suite includes a check that no domain rubric appears in the verify file.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Overlay remains advisory; no domain rubric leaks into code-review-verify.md') has no corresponding described work or test — the eval spec covers recall + no-fire cases but nothing in the plan body describes how the no-leak constraint on code-review-verify.md is verified.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the eval spec section: 'Include a static check (e.g., git diff --name-only restricted to code-review-verify.md returns empty) confirming no changes land in code-review-verify.md.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out step to the plan (e.g., 'revert code-review-security.md to the prior commit if post-merge eval regression is detected') or describe an expand-contract sequence (e.g., shadow-run the enriched overlay before fully replacing the existing one).
findings.finding: The plan modifies an existing overlay file (code-review-security.md) that changes live reviewer behavior, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out plan — only a pre-merge eval gate — leaving no documented path if the enriched overlay regresses production recall or fires in unexpected patterns post-merge.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for AC2 (e.g. a `make eval-security` target or named eval runner script) that exits non-zero on threshold failure, and confirm it runs in CI so the closing PR provides objective evidence of pass/fail.
findings.finding: No proving command or mechanism for executing the recall + no-fire eval is described anywhere in the plan. The eval spec and fixtures are listed as file deliverables but how they are run (script name, CLI invocation, CI step) is absent, making AC2 unverifiable without the executor inventing the tooling.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria – AC2; Files section: 'eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define or cite 'dso' explicitly: name the file path, ADR, or external document that contains the 8 red-team concerns and FP-guards so the enrichment work can be located and verified.
findings.finding: The plan's central content source — 'dso content' and 'dso's 8 red-team concerns' — is referenced as if it is a known, locatable artifact, but 'dso' does not appear anywhere in the codebase, in any doc, ADR, or file. The plan cannot be implemented without knowing what 'dso' refers to and where it lives.
findings.location: ## What — 'Enrich the security overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards … dso's 8 red-team concerns'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan a pointer such as: 'dso = `docs/security/domain-security-overlay.md` (or equivalent)' — or inline the 8 red-team patterns and their FP-guards directly in the plan so the source of truth is unambiguous.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the concrete file path for the eval spec + fixtures (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-security.eval.yaml`) to the Files section so the CREATION target is unambiguous and locatable by CI.
findings.finding: The plan's second named deliverable ('eval spec + fixtures for the security overlay') is a net-new file creation with no concrete path specified, and no such file exists anywhere in the repo (e.g., `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-security.eval.yaml` is absent). The plan omits this path from the Files list, leaving the scope of the CREATION underspecified.
findings.location: Files section / AC: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-security.eval.yaml` to the Files list, following the established `<prompt-id>.eval.yaml` naming convention.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and cite the concrete location of the 'dso' source artifact (file path, ADR number, or external URL) so the recall patterns and FP-guards can be traced to an authoritative, inspectable source.
findings.finding: The plan references 'dso' and 'dso content' (e.g., 'Enrich the security overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards', 'dso's 8 red-team concerns') as though 'dso' is a known, locatable artifact in the repo, but no file, symbol, or directory named 'dso' exists anywhere in the codebase.
findings.location: What section: 'Enrich the security overlay prompt with dso content'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify the origin of the 'dso' 8-concern security taxonomy (external standard, internal ADR, or project document) and add a citation to the plan and to `code-review-security.md`; if it is a novel taxonomy, justify it against an established standard (e.g. OWASP ASVS, MITRE ATT&CK, SANS CWE Top 25) or record the decision in a new ADR.
findings.finding: The plan introduces 'dso's 8 red-team concerns' as a locked, authoritative security taxonomy but provides no prior-art grounding for this custom ontology — neither a reference to an established standard (e.g. OWASP ASVS, MITRE ATT&CK for Enterprise, SANS CWE Top 25, NIST 800-53) nor an internal ADR/doc that defines 'dso'. The term 'dso' does not appear anywhere in the repository.
findings.location: Plan body — 'Recall: dso's 8 red-team concerns (authz completeness, taint flow to sinks, fail-open error handling, privilege escalation via side effects, crypto misuse, TOCTOU, trust boundaries, state-machine integrity)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the concrete file path for the eval spec deliverable to the Files list (e.g. `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-security.eval.yaml`) so the executor and eval harness can locate it.
findings.finding: The eval spec + fixtures deliverable has no concrete file path specified. The repo's established pattern is `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/<prompt-id>.eval.yaml`; no `code-review-security.eval.yaml` exists there, and the plan's Files list gives no path for this second deliverable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-security.eval.yaml` as the explicit path in the Files list.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
